summary: Comprehensive backend testing of HAVEN — 43/43 pytest tests passing across all requested features: health, auth (login/register/me + invalid + dup), cases CRUD + claim + role scoping, tasks/messages/documents (incl. multipart upload + verify), forms CRUD, resources seeded check (>=9), caseworker/admin analytics, BB AI (intro/chat with crisis intent classification, form analyze, form autofill via Claude Sonnet 4.5 + Emergent LLM key), BB application tracking (create + summary), BB Browser Control showstopper (real Playwright headless Chromium: start with screenshot, extract 12+ fields from live DOM at http://localhost:8001/demo/housing-form, fill single field, autofill_all with mapping, navigate, screenshot, stop), and role enforcement (resident blocked from other case, from creating tasks, from deleting forms). All browser sessions were stopped after the test run (verified via /api/bb/browser/sessions returning []).
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py reads JWT_SECRET via os.environ[...] (hard-fail) — good; bcrypt usage correct; tokens carry sub/role/email/iat/exp.

cases_router.list_cases: when caseworker queries without assigned_to_me=true it returns ALL cases unfiltered — fine for caseworker dashboards, but the route does not require_role, so any authenticated non-resident currently returns everything. Consider explicit role gating if you don't want every account type other than resident to see all cases.

bb_router.browser/stop accepts session_id as a query param (good) but does not validate ownership — any authenticated caller could stop another user's session by guessing the id (bb-browser-<user_id>). Low risk because the id is uuid-based, but worth adding an owner check.

case_ops_router.update_task allows any authenticated user (no require_role) to PATCH any task by id, including residents updating tasks not on their own case. Recommend role gating or ownership check.

case_ops_router.list_messages and list_documents don't check that the requesting resident actually owns the case — they would return messages/docs for any case_id given. Add the same resident_id ownership check used in cases_router.get_case.
Documents endpoint stores raw base64 data_url in MongoDB (OK for demo, but flagged in code as 'for demo' — fine for investor demo). 8MB limit enforced.
forms_resources_router.list_forms returns all forms regardless of published flag; intentional for caseworker view — fine.
Pydantic EmailStr rejects .test TLDs (encountered during testing) — already handled by using @haven.demo in tests; user registration in prod with corporate domains is unaffected.
BB chat: persists messages via $setOnInsert + $push — good idempotent upsert pattern.
browser_engine.get_or_create_session uses per-user fixed session_id (bb-browser-<user_id>) — single session per user, simple and correct for demo.
test_credentials: caseworker@haven.demo / resident@haven.demo / admin@haven.demo — all password Demo2026! (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md, confirmed working)
seed_data_creation: No additional seed data created. Test-created data prefixed TEST_ (TEST_task, TEST_message hello, TEST_form, TEST_role_form, TEST_Housing Authority). Form records cleaned up; other TEST_ records left in DB but harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_haven_backend.py runs in ~25s and exercises every endpoint listed in the review request, including the real Playwright BB Browser Control flow against http://localhost:8001/demo/housing-form (12 fields extracted, autofill_all works). Frontend not yet tested per instruction. Code review comments above flag a few minor authorization gaps (update_task/list_messages/list_documents lack ownership checks for residents, browser/stop lacks ownership check) — non-blocking for investor demo but worth addressing.
rca of the issue: N/A — all tests passed. One transient test-author bug (using .test TLD) was fixed in the test file itself.
